evals.prompt: I have a Next.js e-commerce app at /home/seif/test-ecommerce. The checkout flow user story is: 'As a customer, I can add items to my cart, enter shipping info, choose a payment method, and complete my purchase.' I need comprehensive Playwright E2E tests for this. The app runs on port 3000.
evals.expected_output: A comprehensive test suite organized by user story covering happy path, validation, error handling, edge cases, and accessibility. Should use semantic locators, web-first assertions, and mock external payment APIs.
expectations: Tests are organized in describe blocks by behavior (happy path, validation, error handling, etc.)
Happy path test covers the full flow: add to cart -> shipping -> payment -> order confirmation
Validation tests cover empty required fields and invalid inputs
Boundary value tests exist for at least one numeric/string field
External payment API is mocked using page.route()
All locators use semantic methods (getByRole, getByLabel, getByText, getByTestId) — no CSS selectors or XPath
All assertions use web-first auto-retrying methods (toBeVisible, toHaveText, etc.) — no manual isVisible() checks
No hard-coded waits (waitForTimeout)
Tests are independent — no shared state between tests
At least one accessibility test is included (axe-core or keyboard navigation)
A test plan or coverage summary is presented before writing code

evals.prompt: Write playwright tests for our contact form. The form has name, email, message fields and a submit button. After submission it shows a success toast. The project is at /home/seif/test-contact-form and already has playwright installed.
evals.expected_output: Even for this 'simple' form, tests should cover validation boundaries, error states, and accessibility — not just the happy path. Should use data-driven tests for input variations.
expectations: Happy path test submits the form and verifies the success toast appears
Validation tests cover empty fields, invalid email format, and field length boundaries
At least one boundary value test (e.g., name at min/max length, message at min/max length)
Uses parameterized/data-driven tests for input variations instead of copy-pasting
All locators use semantic methods — no CSS selectors or XPath
All assertions use web-first auto-retrying methods
No hard-coded waits
Tests are independent
At least one accessibility check is included
Test names describe the user behavior being verified (not implementation details)
A test plan is presented before writing code

evals.prompt: We already have playwright set up with POM in our project at /home/seif/test-auth-app. There's a pages/ directory with page objects and tests in tests/e2e/. I need E2E tests for the user story: 'As a user, I can sign up, verify my email, and log in.' Make sure to follow our existing patterns.
evals.expected_output: Tests should follow the project's existing POM pattern, extend the base page class, and use the established fixture system. Coverage should include the full signup->verify->login flow plus edge cases.
expectations: New page objects extend the existing base.page.ts pattern
New page objects follow the same style as login.page.ts (method naming, structure)
Tests import from the project's existing fixtures.ts
Happy path covers full flow: signup -> email verification -> login with new credentials
Validation tests for signup fields (password requirements, email format, etc.)
Boundary value test for password length (7, 8, 64, 65 chars given 8-64 requirement)
Error state tests (duplicate email, invalid verification token, etc.)
All locators use semantic methods
All assertions use web-first auto-retrying methods
Tests match the file naming convention of the existing tests
A test plan is presented before writing code
